subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Tycho Brahe: The Observer King
description: A 60-second lesson on Tycho Brahe: The Observer King.

hook.text: Tycho Brahe measured star positions more accurately than anyone before him—all without a telescope.

buildup.text: He built Uraniborg, a palace-sized observatory in Denmark, funded by the king. It was the finest in the world.

discovery.text: His data on Mars's orbit was the most precise ever collected. It would later prove essential to Kepler's laws.

twist.text: Tycho had a gold-and-silver nose prosthesis—he lost part of his nose in a sword duel over a math argument.

climax.text: He hired Kepler as his assistant but guarded his data jealously. Only Tycho's death gave Kepler full access.

punchline.text: A golden nose and the best data in history. One man's obsession changed astronomy.

quiz.question: What was Tycho Brahe's most important contribution to astronomy?
options: Extremely precise naked-eye measurements of planetary positions, especially Mars
Discovering that stars are actually distant suns
